_meta.description: Historical base rates for Kalshi prediction market categories. All priors are in the 0-100 scale (matching Kalshi yes-price convention). Sources: FOMC historical records, BLS/BEA data, NOAA climate normals, sports historical outcomes.

fed_rate_hike.description: Fed funds rate hike at any given FOMC meeting (post-2009 era). Historically rare outside of tightening cycles.
fed_rate_hike.context_notes: Conditional on tightening cycle: ~30-40%. Neutral/easing cycle: ~2-5%. This prior is the unconditional long-run average.

fed_rate_hold.description: Fed funds rate held unchanged at any given FOMC meeting (post-2009 era). Most common outcome.
fed_rate_hold.context_notes: Dominant outcome in most regimes. Near-zero floor era (2009-2015, 2020-2022): ~90%.

fed_rate_cut.description: Fed funds rate cut at any given FOMC meeting (post-2009 era). More common than hikes in crisis/easing periods.
fed_rate_cut.context_notes: Conditional on easing cycle: ~50-70%. This prior is the unconditional average.

cpi_above_consensus.description: CPI (headline or core) comes in above Bloomberg consensus estimate. Roughly a coin flip but with slight upward bias since 2021.

cpi_below_consensus.description: CPI (headline or core) comes in below Bloomberg consensus estimate.

nfp_above_consensus.description: Non-farm payrolls (NFP) comes in above consensus estimate. Slight historical upward bias due to positive revision culture.
nfp_above_consensus.context_notes: 2010-2024: ~54%. BLS frequently revises initial prints upward.

nfp_below_consensus.description: Non-farm payrolls (NFP) comes in below consensus estimate.

gdp_above_consensus.description: GDP (advance or revision) comes in above consensus estimate. Near coin flip historically.
gdp_above_consensus.context_notes: Advance GDP estimates have wide consensus dispersion; beat/miss near 50/50.

weather_extreme_heat.description: Temperature exceeds a high threshold (e.g. above 100F or +2 SD above seasonal mean) on a given day/week. Summer prior for hot cities.
weather_extreme_heat.context_notes: Highly location- and season-dependent. This is a generic summer prior for warm-climate US cities (Phoenix, Miami). Northern cities in summer: ~10%.

weather_extreme_cold.description: Temperature falls below a low threshold on a given day/week. Winter prior for cold cities.
weather_extreme_cold.context_notes: Winter prior for northern US cities (Chicago, Minneapolis). Southern cities in winter: ~5%.

presidential_incumbent_win.description: Incumbent party wins presidential election or primary. Historical incumbency advantage.
presidential_incumbent_win.context_notes: Post-WWII US: incumbent party wins ~56% of general elections. Primary incumbency: ~85%.

sports_home_team_win.description: Home team wins in major US sports. Home field advantage varies by sport.
sports_home_team_win.context_notes: NFL: ~57%. NBA: ~60%. MLB: ~54%. NHL: ~55%. Using NFL/general average.

sports_favorite_win.description: Betting favorite wins the game/match. Generic prior when favorite is identified.
sports_favorite_win.context_notes: Vegas favorites win roughly 60-65% of games across major sports.

debt_ceiling_raised.description: US debt ceiling is raised/suspended before default. Has always been raised historically.
debt_ceiling_raised.context_notes: Never actually defaulted. Last-minute deals are the norm. Very high prior.

government_shutdown.description: US federal government shuts down at a funding deadline.
government_shutdown.context_notes: Shutdowns happen but are not the modal outcome at any given deadline. ~20-25% of funding deadlines result in a shutdown.

bill_passes_congress.description: A specific bill passes both chambers and is signed into law. Most bills die in committee.
bill_passes_congress.context_notes: Only ~4-6% of introduced bills become law. Bills with momentum: ~20-30%.

pce_above_consensus.description: PCE inflation (Fed preferred measure) comes in above consensus.
pce_above_consensus.context_notes: Similar to CPI beat rate. Slight correlation with CPI surprises.

unemployment_above_consensus.description: Unemployment rate comes in above consensus estimate (higher unemployment = miss for labor market).
unemployment_above_consensus.context_notes: Unemployment is a lagging indicator. Near coin flip. Slightly lower prior (labor market tends to be stronger than expected in recoveries).
